Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: dental arch defect.
Examination: Teeth 15, 44, and 46 are missing, with well-healed gingiva, adequate keratinized gingiva, and good alveolar ridge form without significant depression. Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint function. Teeth 26, 34, and 36 have sutures in place with well-healed wounds.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 15 and 46 show adequate bone volume and quality. Tooth 44 shows adequate bone quality but insufficient volume.
Diagnosis: Teeth 15, 26, 34, 36, 44, and 46: dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: Implant-supported prostheses for teeth 15, 44, and 46. Suture removal for teeth 34 and 36.
Management: For teeth 15, 44, and 46, informed consent was obtained, and the patient signed the consent form for implant restoration. After routine disinfection, local infiltration anesthesia with 4% articaine was administered. Circular incisions were made at the crest of the alveolar ridge for tooth 15, and horizontal incisions for teeth 44 and 46. Full-thickness flaps were elevated, and osteotomies were prepared to the planned depth. A BEGO implant (3.75x10mm) was placed in tooth 15 with a healing abutment (4.5x5mm), and BEGO implants (3.75x10mm and 4.5x10mm) were placed in teeth 44 and 46 with cover screws. Tension-free suturing was performed, and radiographic verification was satisfactory. Sutures were removed from teeth 34 and 36 after disinfection.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if any discomfort occurs. Avoid brushing or rinsing for 24 hours. Begin eating 2 hours post-procedure, avoiding hot foods.
Remarks:

Oral hygiene is average. Normal mouth opening and temporomandibular joint movement. No other abnormalities noted.
Radiographic Examination: Adequate bone volume and quality observed for teeth 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26.
Diagnosis: Single arch edentulism (K08.101). Maxillary arch edentulism.
Treatment Plan: Placement of ALL-ON-6 implant-supported prosthesis for teeth 11, 14, 16, 21, 24, 26.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For teeth 11, 14, 16, 21, 24, 26, the patient was fully informed and consented by signing the oral implant restoration consent form. Preoperative antibiotics were administered. Standard disinfection and draping were performed. Local infiltration anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made along the alveolar ridge, followed by a full-thickness flap reflection. Implant sites were prepared to the planned depth, and Dentium implants were placed: 3.4*12mm for teeth 11, 21; 3.8*12mm for teeth 14, 24; 4.3*10mm for teeth 16, 26. Abutments were placed on teeth 11, 21, 14, 24, and cover screws on teeth 16, 26. Bone grafting and membrane coverage were performed. Tension-free suturing was completed, and postoperative radiographs confirmed implant positioning. The procedure lasted from 10:20 to 11:40. Impressions were taken for transitional prosthesis fabrication, and occlusion was adjusted and polished.
Medical Instructions: Strictly follow postoperative implant care instructions. Take oral antibiotics as prescribed. Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Suture removal in two weeks.
Remarks:
